meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Lebanese resident stays behind with pet monkey despite evacuation orders
items.summary: A Lebanese man has refused to leave Tyre in southern Lebanon, staying with his pet monkey despite evacuation orders.

items.title: Iran’s IRGC claims attacks on UAE, Bahrain aluminium facilities
items.summary: Iran targets Aluminium Bahrain, Emirates Global Aluminium in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks on its infrastructure.

items.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Japanese GP to become youngest F1 title leader
items.summary: The 19-year-old Mercedes driver's historic championship accomplishment came after he won his second F1 race in a row.

items.title: ‘Trump has to open the Strait of Hormuz’ with ground forces
items.summary: Kenneth Katzman says US war objectives in Iran that cannot be met with air power can be met with a ground invasion.

items.title: Thick plumes of smoke rise over Iraq’s Mosul after strikes
items.summary: Thick plumes of smoke rise over Mosul after air strikes targeted sites belonging to Iraq’s Popular Mobilisation Forces.

items.title: Toddler rescued after US-Israeli strike on Iran
items.summary: Rescue teams from the Iranian Red Crescent rescued a toddler from a destroyed building after a US-Israeli strike.

items.title: Belgium thrash USA 5-2; Mexico vs Portugal ends 0-0 in football friendlies
items.summary: A man died after falling from the stands at Azteca Stadium, where Mexico hosted Portugal with Ronaldo missing the match.

items.title: Israel Adesanya knocked out by Joe Pyfer at UFC Fight Night in Seattle
items.summary: Former two-time champion loses his fourth straight bout after being stopped by Pyfer in the second round.

items.title: Sabalenka defeats Gauff to win second straight Miami Open title
items.summary: World number one Aryna Sabalenka edges Coco Gauff in a tense three-set final to claim the 'Sunshine Double' in Florida.

items.title: What happens in the occupied West Bank under the shadow of the Iran war
items.summary: Since Israel went to war with Iran, settler violence in the occupied West Bank has surged.

items.title: As war on Iran enters second month, Yemen’s Houthis open new front
items.summary: The Yemeni group pledges more attacks on Israel as fears mount that the worsening conflict will spiral out of control.

items.title: Iran war live: Pakistan, Turkiye, Egypt, Saudi seek to de-escalate
items.summary: Push for diplomacy as Iran threatens retaliatory attacks on Israeli, US universities in the Middle East.

items.title: Vice President JD Vance tops CPAC’s straw poll to be US president in 2028
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, however, made advances in this year's informal poll about who might succeed Trump.

items.title: Key moments in first month of US-Israeli war on Iran
items.summary: One month after US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, the conflict has escalated across the Middle East.

items.title: Air strikes in Iraq kill three PMF fighters, two police
items.summary: Iraq turning into 'expanding battleground' in war, which began on February 28 with US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

items.title: Reza Pahlavi pledges to ‘make Iran great again’ at 2026 CPAC conference
items.summary: Iranian opposition leader calls on Trump administration to 'stay the course' as the US and Israel wage war on Iran.

items.title: Pakistan secures Iran deal to send 20 ships through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Islamabad's diplomatic push bears fruit as the world watches for signs of a broader breakthrough.

items.title: Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drone strikes
items.summary: Thick, black smoke rose from Kuwait International Airport Saturday after suspected Iranian drone strikes caused damage.

items.title: Could Ukraine’s drone expertise serve Gulf countries?
items.summary: Gulf nations seek new defence partnerships as Iran war escalates.

items.title: Palestine Action supporters arrested as London’s Met Police reverse policy
items.summary: Arrests come days after force announced U-turn, saying that despite High Court ruling, 'terror' ban remains in place.

items.title: Missiles hit southern Israeli city of Eilat
items.summary: Israeli social media posts show a strike in the Israeli city of Eilat.

items.title: Thousands march through London in stand against the far right
items.summary: Organisers say 'Together Alliance' event was the largest anti-far-right march in British history.

items.title: ‘No Kings’ protests erupt across the US, with a Minnesota focus
items.summary: Saturday's rallies mark the third round of 'No Kings' protests since President Trump took office for a second term.

items.title: As war rages, Iranian politicians push for exit from nuclear weapons treaty
items.summary: While US-Israeli attacks hit key infrastructure, hardliners demand withdrawal from Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.

items.title: France opens probe into suspected attack on Bank of America in Paris
items.summary: Police arrest suspect before setting off explosive device outside US bank's headquarters.
